title: alchemilla vulgaris var. demissa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. demissa is a variety of the common lady's mantle, a flowering plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which provide health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating conditions like eczema, acne, and wounds. Herbal preparations of Alchemilla vulgaris var. demissa include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally to promote overall health and well-being.
